meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Who actually runs Iran right now? The key power players as Trump claims talks to 'top' official
items.summary: Analysts say the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has emerged as Iran's dominant force, with Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's grip on power reportedly uncertain after recent strikes.

items.title: US military sends drones, alongside 200 troops, to Nigeria amid fears of renewed Boko Haram insurgency
items.summary: The U.S. military deployed MQ-9 Reaper drones to Nigeria for intelligence-gathering and training as the African country is grappling with a security crisis.

items.title: North Korean dictator says government will keep cementing nation's 'irreversible status as a nuclear power'
items.summary: North Korean figure Kim Jong Un said the government will bolster the nation's "irreversible status as a nuclear power," The Associated Press reported.

items.title: NATO scrambles jets as Russia fires nearly 400 drones toward Ukraine, signaling new spring offensive
items.summary: Russia launched nearly 400 drones and missiles at Ukraine, prompting Poland and Romania to scramble NATO fighter jets as strikes neared their airspace.

items.title: Defiant Iran vows to fight 'until complete victory,' despite heavy military losses
items.summary: An Iranian military spokesman said Tuesday that Tehran's armed forces will battle "until complete victory," despite the U.S. reporting heavy losses for them.

items.title: Colombian military plane crash kills at least 66, head of armed forces says
items.summary: A Colombian military transport plane crashed near Puerto Leguizamo, Colombia, shortly after takeoff, killing at least 66 people and injuring dozens.

items.title: 'Americas Counter Cartel Coalition': Inside the US strategy to combat narco terror, confront China, other foes
items.summary: Joseph M. Humire, acting assistant secretary of War for Homeland Defense and Americas Security Affairs, explains the Trump administration's strategy for the Western Hemisphere.

items.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine has evidence Russia is aiding Iran with intelligence
items.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, whose nation has been at war with Russia for years, has indicated that there is evidence that Russia is supplying Iran with intelligence.

items.title: Iran conflict tests Pakistan amid own border clashes as Islamabad touted as venue for US-Tehran talks
items.summary: Pakistan, the only nuclear-armed Muslim state, faces mounting pressure as its Saudi defense pact and Iran ties clash amid the intensifying regional war.

items.title: UK counterterrorism police probe antisemitic arson attack as Iran-linked group claims responsibility
items.summary: An Iran-linked group claims responsibility for torching Jewish ambulances outside a Golders Green synagogue in London as counterterrorism police investigate.

items.title: EU blocks US vote to define gender as biological men and women at UN women’s forum
items.summary: The EU used a procedural motion to block a U.S. resolution at the United Nations that sought to define gender as referring to biological men and women, leaving it without a vote.

items.title: If Cuba falls, who steps in? Castro dynasty shadows island’s future
items.summary: As President Donald Trump signals possible action, experts say Cuba has no clear successor while Raúl Castro, 94, remains a significant influence on the communist country.

items.title: Trump, Starmer agree Strait of Hormuz must reopen as Middle East conflict escalates
items.summary: Strait of Hormuz closure dominates a Trump-Starmer call, as Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to Iran warns of further U.S. strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure.

items.title: Iran threatens mass ‘water war’ with strikes on key plants in days, UN official warns
items.summary: Iran threatens to target desalination and energy infrastructure within days, a U.N. official warns, citing lasting consequences for global markets and water supplies.

items.title: Authorities share update on circumstances around missing college student Jimmy Gracey's death
items.summary: University of Alabama student Jimmy Gracey, 20, went missing in Barcelona during Spring Break. His body was found near a nightclub.

items.title: UK nuclear submarine deployed to Arabian Sea before Iran targets key US-UK base: reports
items.summary: Iranian FM Seyed Abbas Araghchi warned Britain against supporting U.S. military action, calling its decision to allow U.S. forces to use its bases "participation in aggression."

items.title: Iran chokes Strait of Hormuz with reported $2M tanker toll, regime threatens global oil supply
items.summary: Strait of Hormuz traffic is near collapse as Iran reportedly charges tankers $2 million in transit fees, prompting President Trump to threaten strikes on Iranian power infrastructure.

items.title: Carlos Fernández de Cossío: Cuba 'preparing' for 'possibility of military aggression'
items.summary: Cuba's deputy foreign minister says the military is preparing for possible U.S. aggression after Trump said it would be an "honor" to take Cuba.

items.title: Analysts say Gaza 'civilian' deaths include Hamas, other terror members working as medics, media workers
items.summary: Researchers say Hamas and PIJ martyr lists reveal Gaza terrorists who posed as doctors, nurses and journalists, clouding civilian casualty figures.

items.title: Churchill, Shakespeare and the UK flag all under siege in modern Britain, commentators say
items.summary: Britain's statues, flags and icons like Churchill and Shakespeare are under attack, as commentators warn DEI ideology is erasing the nation's history.

items.title: Dad loses custody of autistic son after fighting sex change, gets support from Elon Musk
items.summary: Father in Iceland says he lost custody after opposing his 11-year-old autistic son's sex reassignment, claiming courts prioritized ideology over parental rights.

items.title: Trump proven right on Iran's long-range missile capability as regime targets US-UK base, experts say
items.summary: Experts say Iran's Diego Garcia missile launch exposes deception about range limits, and analysts warn Europe now faces a direct ballistic threat.

items.title: Iranian man, Romanian woman charged after allegedly trying to enter UK nuclear missile base, officials say
items.summary: An Iranian man and Romanian woman have been charged after allegedly trying to enter HM Naval Base Clyde, the U.K.'s nuclear submarine base in Scotland.

items.title: Families of Iran's elite live lavishly abroad while ordinary citizens suffer at home
items.summary: Critics say children of Iran's ruling elite live freely in the West while their families enforce strict rules at home, with calls to sanction them.

items.title: Live possum discovered hiding among plush toys in an Australian airport gift shop
items.summary: A wild possum found a clever hiding spot among plush toys in an airport gift shop in Australia and was safely removed, staff confirmed.
